pull.title: fix: tinygemm2 hang issue due to barrier sync
pull.body:
<!-- .github/pull_request_template.md -->

## 📌 Description

The `tinygemm2` kernel hung intermittently on H100 CI runners. Running `compute-sanitizer --tool synccheck` confirmed a barrier synchronization failure.

## Root Cause

The kernel launches 384 threads (12 warps of 32):

| Warps | Threads   | Role                                      |
|-------|-----------|-------------------------------------------|
| 0-3   | 0-127     | Compute (HMMA + reduction)                |
| 4-7   | 128-255   | DMA -- weight TMA (1 elected per warp)    |
| 8-11  | 256-383   | DMA -- activation TMA (1 elected per warp)|

The original control flow in `csrc/tinygemm2.cu` was:

```cpp
// BEFORE (buggy)
if (warp_id >= 4 && elect_one_sync()) {
    // DMA work -- only 8 elected threads enter
}
else if (warp_id < 4) {
    // Compute work -- 128 threads enter
    reduction_buffer[threadIdx.x] = accum4;
    __syncthreads();          // <-- BUG: only warps 0-3 reach this
    if (warp_id == 0) { /* reduction + output */ }
}
```

**248 out of 384 threads** (all of warps 4-11, elected or not) never reached
`__syncthreads()`. Per the CUDA spec, `__syncthreads()` requires **all threads
in the CTA** to participate. Violating this is undefined behavior -- on some
H100 configurations it silently works, on others the block hangs forever.

## Fix

### File: `csrc/tinygemm2.cu` (lines 198-391)

Two structural changes, no logic changes:

**1. Separate the warp-id check from the election** so non-elected DMA threads
   fall through to `__syncthreads()`:

```cpp
// AFTER (fixed)
if (warp_id >= 4) {
    // Outer block: ALL threads in warps 4-11 enter here.
    // Non-elected threads skip the inner block and fall through.
    if (elect_one_sync()) {
        // DMA work -- only 8 elected threads
    }
}
else {                          // warp_id < 4
    // Compute work -- 128 threads
    reduction_buffer[threadIdx.x] = accum4;
}
```

**2. Move `__syncthreads()` and the warp-0 reduction outside the if/else**
   so all 384 threads participate:

```cpp
// AFTER (fixed) -- after the if/else block closes
__syncthreads();                // ALL 384 threads reach this

if (warp_id == 0) {
    // reduction + output (unchanged)
}
```

## 🔍 Related Issues

<!-- Link any related issues here -->

## 🚀 Pull Request Checklist

Thank you for contributing to FlashInfer! Before we review your pull request, please make sure the following items are complete.

### ✅ Pre-commit Checks

- [ ] I have installed `pre-commit` by running `pip install pre-commit` (or used your preferred method).
- [ ] I have installed the hooks with `pre-commit install`.
- [ ] I have run the hooks manually with `pre-commit run --all-files` and fixed any reported issues.

> If you are unsure about how to set up `pre-commit`, see [the pre-commit documentation](https://pre-commit.com/).

## 🧪 Tests

- [ ] Tests have been added or updated as needed.
- [ ] All tests are passing (`unittest`, etc.).

## Reviewer Notes

<!-- Optional: anything you'd like reviewers to focus on, concerns, etc. -->


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
  * Clarified and strengthened GPU kernel synchronization and control flow to improve stability and correctness of computations.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Relaxed a strict input tile constraint so more valid input shapes are accepted.

* **Chores**
  * Re-enabled tinygemm2 tests across CI; tests now perform runtime capability checks and use reduced/adjusted parameter sets for faster, more reliable execution.

             f"out.shape[1] ({out.shape[1]}) must equal weight.shape[0] ({weight.shape[0]})"
         )
-
-    input_features = input.shape[1]
     output_features = weight.shape[0]
 
-    if input_features % 64 != 0:
-        raise ValueError(
-            f"input_features ({input_features}) must be a multiple of 64 (TMA swizzle constraint)"
-        )
     if output_features % 16 != 0:
         raise ValueError(
             f"output_features ({output_features}) must be a multiple of 16 (tile alignment)"
